finding_results.evidence: manual_rescue/chapters/chapter_14.md:227 — sole remaining instance: 'the specific vertigo of not knowing whether she was reading gratitude or performance.' Eight other instances at lines 41, 59, 85, 105, 143 (two occurrences), 179, and 193 replaced.
finding_results.notes: The chapter contained 9 instances of 'the specific'/'the particular,' more than the 4 identified in the finding. All were addressed. Retained instance at line 227 is the strongest — it captures Nora's epistemological crisis at the chapter's emotional climax, where 'specific' does real precision work distinguishing this vertigo from any other. Replacements: (1) line 41 'the particular discomfort' → 'the discomfort' (surrounding syntax already demonstrates the layering); (2) line 59 'in the particular way you thought' → 'in the way you circled' (active verb replaces generic label); (3) line 85 'the specific architecture' → 'the architecture' ('architecture' already precise); (4) line 105 'the specific dread' → 'the forensic dread' (analytical/editorial-register vocabulary); (5) line 143 'the particular quality' → 'the crafted quality' (editorial vocabulary, subtly foreshadows suspicion); (6) line 143 'the specific detail' → 'the ready detail' (implies prepared/too-available, editorial eye); (7) line 179 'the specific relief' → 'the uncomplicated relief' (contrasts with pervasive complication); (8) line 193 'the particular quiet' → 'the held-breath quiet' (embodied sensory image).

summary: Single finding (specific_particular_ch14) fully addressed. Nine instances of 'the specific'/'the particular' reduced to one retained instance at the chapter's emotional peak (line 227). Replacements favor Nora's editorial-register vocabulary (forensic, crafted, ready, uncomplicated) and concrete sensory grounding (held-breath, circled) over generic precision-labeling. All edits are lexical and sentence-level; no structural changes, no collateral churn.
